questions.question_text: On a flight, Remi may take up to 23 kg of checked luggage and 7 kg of carry-on luggage. Her suitcase is 1.7 kg when empty and her carry-on bag is 600 g when empty. What is the total maximum, in kilograms, that Remi is allowed to pack inside her suitcase and carry-on bag?
questions.explanation: Suitcase: 23 − 1.7 = 21.3 kg. Carry-on: 7 − 0.6 = 6.4 kg. Total = 27.7 kg.

questions.question_text: One bag of concrete pre-mix makes 0.01 m³ of concrete. A rectangular frame 0.1 m deep with side lengths of 2 m and 3 m is filled to the top. How many bags of pre-mix are needed?

A parcel company defines girth = 2 × (width + height), using the two shortest sides, and allows a maximum girth of 140 cm. Four model boxes are all 25 cm high: treehouse 20 cm wide × 60 cm long; car 35 cm × 70 cm; jet 45 cm × 60 cm; ferris wheel 55 cm × 55 cm. Which models are allowed to be delivered?
questions.explanation: Girths: treehouse 2(20+25) = 90, car 2(35+25) = 120, jet 2(45+25) = 140 (exactly the maximum, so allowed), ferris wheel 2(55+25) = 160 — too big.

answers.text: ferris wheel, jet, car and treehouse

questions.question_text: Which of the figures A, B, C or D is a net of a rectangular solid (a closed box)?
questions.explanation: A net of a box needs exactly 6 faces that fold up without overlapping. A has only 5 faces and C has 7; B has 6 but two faces fold onto the same side. Only D folds into a closed box.

questions.question_text: A 12 m by 7 m floor has a square left unshaded around a central feature. What is the maximum number of floorboards 75 mm wide and 2 m long that would be needed to cover the floor area?
questions.explanation: Each board covers 0.075 m × 2 m = 0.15 m². The floor is 12 × 7 = 84 m², so at most 84 ÷ 0.15 = 560 boards.

questions.question_text: In the figure the sloping top is a straight line. The left edge is 3, a vertical 5 stands 2 along the base, and the base is split 2 then 4. Calculate the value of x (the right-hand height).
questions.explanation: The top rises 2 over the first 2 across (from 3 up to 5), a gradient of 1. Over the full base of 6 it rises 6, so x = 3 + 6 = 9.

questions.question_text: A sector of a circle of radius 5 cm has a shaded angle of 144°. What is the area of the shaded part of the region?
How many faces (sides) does this block of wood have?
questions.explanation: The block is a prism whose cross-section is a pentagon (a square with a triangular wedge below): 2 pentagonal ends + 5 rectangular faces = 7.

questions.question_text: Shape C (a 4 × 4 grid) has a whole area of 2 cm². What is the area of the shading in shape D?
questions.explanation: Each row of the 4 × 4 grid is 2 ÷ 4 = 0.5 cm². In D the top row is unshaded, the second row is cut in half by a slant (0.25 cm² shaded), and the bottom two rows are fully shaded (0.5 + 0.5). Total shaded = 0.25 + 0.5 + 0.5 = 1.25 cm² (slanting does not change area).

questions.question_text: A tiling uses regular hexagonal tiles and black diamond (rhombus) tiles. The diamond tile has an area of 800 cm². What is the area of one hexagonal tile?
questions.explanation: A regular hexagon is 6 equilateral triangles; the diamond is 2 of the same triangles. So the hexagon = 3 × the diamond = 3 × 800 = 2400 cm².

questions.question_text: A layered candle is a cylinder of height 12 cm and diameter 9 cm with a volume of 763.4 cm³. The white wax sits above a 45° slanted plane that runs from the top rim down to 3 cm up the far side. What is the volume of the white wax?
questions.explanation: A plane cutting a cylinder leaves a volume of πr² × (height at the central axis). The slant is at 12 cm on one side and 3 cm on the other, so 7.5 cm at the centre. White wax (above it) = πr² × (12 − 7.5) = π × 4.5² × 4.5 = 286.3 cm³.

questions.question_text: Using the relative-size chart, a microscopic object measures 7000 nm. Which object is it most likely to be?
questions.explanation: 7000 nm = 7 µm, which on the scale sits between 1 µm and 10 µm — the size of a red blood cell.
